description: Market-structure scores are directional 1-5 reads for market-map strategy: attractiveness and growth are better when higher; competition, resource intensity, and distribution difficulty are harder when higher.

markets.name: Prediction-Market Prime Brokerage
markets.summary: Cross-venue APIs are emerging, but unified collateral, margin, custody, and settlement for event contracts remain underbuilt.
markets.profit_wedge: Start with funded-account orchestration, risk limits, and position/margin views before attempting true cross-venue clearing.

markets.name: Neutral Event-Market Data Vendor
markets.summary: Prediction markets need clean reference data, contract metadata, histories, resolution criteria, and cross-venue normalization.
markets.profit_wedge: The wedge is not prices alone; it is canonical contract identity, settlement/rules metadata, and historical curves suitable for backtesting.

markets.name: Retail Brokerage & Execution
markets.summary: Huge pools of users and assets, but customer acquisition, regulation, payment-for-order-flow economics, and incumbent brands dominate.
markets.profit_wedge: Niche audiences, embedded brokerage, or a new asset class beat launching another generic equities broker.

markets.name: Market Data Infrastructure
markets.summary: Ticker plants, feed handlers, and low-latency market data are lucrative but dominated by incumbents, exchange licenses, and colocation economics.
markets.profit_wedge: Wedges exist around underserved asset classes, developer ergonomics, and derived analytics, not core exchange feed redistribution.

markets.name: Alternative Data Vendors
markets.summary: Funds will pay for alpha, but signal decay, data rights, compliance review, and procurement friction make repeatable scale hard.
markets.profit_wedge: Own a proprietary source with durable rights, or sell workflow/context instead of a one-off alpha feed.

markets.name: Institutional Crypto Market Infrastructure
markets.summary: Institutions want custody, execution, credit, settlement, and risk across venues, but trust and regulatory posture separate winners.
markets.profit_wedge: Prime brokerage, portfolio margin, collateral mobility, and compliance tooling are stronger wedges than another exchange connector.

markets.name: Indices & Benchmarks
markets.summary: Benchmarks compound through brand, regulation, ETF assets, and licensing, but new entrants need a new asset class or distribution channel.
markets.profit_wedge: Build benchmarks for emerging markets, crypto, private assets, or prediction/event curves where incumbent index brands have not yet standardized the category.

markets.name: Clearing, Custody & Settlement
markets.summary: Post-trade infrastructure can be extremely durable, but licenses, risk capital, regulation, and trust make it hard to enter.
markets.profit_wedge: The best new wedges are in new asset classes or interoperability, not directly competing with OCC/DTCC on mature instruments.

markets.name: Strategy & Asset-Management Platforms
markets.summary: Model marketplaces, strategy wrappers, and managed products can scale, but trust, performance, regulation, and distribution dominate.
markets.profit_wedge: Start with a trusted niche or embedded distribution; generic strategy marketplaces tend to become performance-chasing churn machines.
